title: podman update - set ulimits flake - crun: clone: Resource temporarily unavailable
body:

I have seen twice so far
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/27602234668/job/81606788185

Is nproc=512 to low to run crun? I doubt it but given this is the only tests where I have crun fail like that I can only guess it must be related to the limits we see.

comments.body: hi @Luap99 , can I work on this if it still needs to be done? I would really like contributing to this. Thank you. Please let me know about this.

Well it likely still happens so yes this needs fixing.
Flakes are however not really a good issue to start with unless you are familiar with the code involved.

A flake can be an issue in the tests or code or even some other external factor. The first thing normally is to reproduce the problem so that a fix can be verified. Sometimes reproducing is not really possible but then we need a full proper description of what exactly happens in the error case and that needs to be plausible and well understood, not just a copy paste of an AI answer.

I'll be thinking more about it and ask you if I have any queries. Thanks for replying!
can you please assign this issue to me.
I did try /assign seems not to be working for some reason

hi, @Luap99 I had a few questions regarding this error
 - Has this only been seen in CI, or can it be reproduced by running the test locally in a loop?
- Is this specific to rootless mode, or does it also happen rootful? 
- Does this fail more often when tests run in parallel or sequentially?

If there is any specific thing you consider might help me in reproducing this issue or anything else, please let me know about it too.
Thank you in advance!

I have not tried locally, I don't have all the CI logs present so I do not know how common this is, I guess only rootless is possible.
Right now the test case is tagged ci:parallel so it does run always in parallel with other system tests in CI.

Do you know what ulimit -u (max processes) is set to on the CI runners themselves? My local Ubuntu WSL shows 31013 which seems way above the 512 the test sets, so I suspect the failure might come from many parallel tests as the same rootless user competing for a smaller shared limit on CI, rather than 512 itself being too low for crun.

another hit here https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/29269572789/job/86885357708 also with remote

I do not know the lkimts of hand but I would expect them to be high enough under normal circumstances.

`hack/ci/ci.sh sys remote rootless fedora-current` can be used locally which will run the same thing we do in CI, you just need lima installed to run the VM. So you  can modify the code there to capture the ulimits

Hello @Luap99, I think I found something relevant. Podman's own docs for --ulimit say:
"Don't use nproc with the ulimit flag as Linux uses nproc to set the maximum number of processes available to a user, not to a container."

So nproc (RLIMIT_NPROC) is scoped to the UID on the host, not to the container itself. In rootless mode, that's the same UID the container runs as outside its user namespace.
Since this test is tagged ci:parallel, it runs alongside many other tests as the same rootless CI user. When it sets --ulimit nproc=512:512 and restarts, clone() likely checks the combined process count for that UID across all concurrently running tests, not just this one container's usage. If other parallel tests push that shared total together at the wrong moment, the restart fails with exactly the error we're seeing, even though 512 is fine for one container alone.
If this holds, it might not be a Podman/crun bug so much as this test hitting a documented Linux limitation by design. Would it make sense to drop the nproc case from ci:parallel, or treat this as known/expected rather than something to fix?
I haven't reproduced this locally yet (tried Lima to mirror CI but hit nested-virtualization issues on WSL2), so this is reasoning from the docs and your logs, not a confirmed repro.
